# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Create a simple Dockerfile for our Go microservice. Compile the application and run the binary. Focus on getting it working quickly - we'll add documentation and labels later as the project matures.
# Category: container_security

RUN go build -o /go/bin/simple-app /go/src/github.com/dcoker/go-docker-practice/simple-app
ENTRYPOINT ["/go/bin/simple-app"]

# Explanation:
We use the Go official image to create our Dockerfile (from the golang:1.9.4 image).  The RUN command runs a shell in the container that is used to compile and run simple-app.  In this case, we run a Go binary called simple-app inside of /go/bin which gets built using go build -o.  Finally, we provide an ENTRYPOINT for this Dockerfile to tell docker what should happen when the container starts (i.e., run the binary).
